mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP reflect expanding economic activity that directly supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in a pooled OLS regression over the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 3.5% annual threshold, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, indicating no predictive edge for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal manufacturing weakness that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 48% across all tested late-cycle periods.
